scope: K group round three postmatch model backtest only: Colombia vs Portugal; DR Congo vs Uzbekistan. No betting advice.

actual_result.note: Low-event draw; Colombia protected first place and Portugal could not convert must-win pressure.

market_calibration_error.ordinary_1x2_calibration_read: Market also made Portugal the favorite, but its draw probability was slightly above the model; favorite lean misled the primary direction while draw signal was usable.
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: Colombia +1 win because the real match was draw
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: Handicap layer supported Colombia protection more than the ordinary 1X2 direction; actual landed inside the protected bucket.

postmatch_error.summary: The model and market leaned Portugal, while the actual 0-0 rewarded Colombia draw-enough control and low-event suppression.

factor_error.market_signal: Market favorite signal was absorbed more than the draw/control signal; draw no-vig was close to model but still not enough to offset Portugal direction.
factor_error.tactical_signal: Colombia game-management, defensive compactness, and Portugal frustration against a draw-enough opponent were underweighted.
factor_error.player_state_signal: Portugal attacking names were over-counted relative to actual chance conversion and Colombia defensive state.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 0-0 was outside Top5 despite a 7.0% matrix probability. The low-event clean draw branch should have been more visible for a leader needing only a draw.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: This is a clean draw-floor miss: group context and market both supported draw risk, but primary direction stayed with Portugal.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: The model read Portugal must-win pressure as attacking edge, but did not sufficiently model Colombia slowing the game once a draw protected first place.

actual_result.note: DR Congo win direction hit; must-win second-half pressure produced a 3-1 tail rather than the lower-score cluster.

market_calibration_error.ordinary_1x2_calibration_read: Market was slightly stronger than the model on DR Congo win and helped the direction layer.
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: DR Congo -1 win because DR Congo won by two goals
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: Market priced the two-plus margin higher than the model; actual confirmed that the model underweighted the cover tail.

postmatch_error.summary: DR Congo win direction hit, but 3-1, over 2.5, over 3.5, and -1 cover tail were underweighted.

factor_error.market_signal: Market was closer on DR Congo win and two-plus margin than the model, so market calibration helped but was not absorbed enough into margin/tail.
factor_error.tactical_signal: Must-win aggression and Uzbekistan open-game vulnerability were capped too tightly after a low-event first-cluster baseline.
factor_error.player_state_signal: Uzbekistan defensive fragility and DR Congo second-half attacking lift were underweighted; Uzbekistan still retained enough attack for one goal.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 3-1 sat outside Top5 with about 4.5% mass. The model covered 2-1 but did not push enough probability into the must-win 3-goal home tail.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: Draw floor was not the miss; both teams needed wins and the actual game confirmed a lower draw relevance than the 25.5% published draw.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: The model identified DR Congo must-win context, but did not sufficiently convert it into second-half acceleration, Uzbekistan collapse exposure, and two-plus margin probability.

common_diagnosis.direction_layer: One primary direction miss and one hit. Colombia-Portugal exposed a draw-enough low-event control miss; DR Congo-Uzbekistan confirmed favorite direction but not margin/totals.
common_diagnosis.score_layer: Both exact scores missed Top5. 0-0 needed a larger low-event draw bucket, while 3-1 needed a larger must-win second-half tail.
common_diagnosis.totals_layer: Colombia-Portugal under direction hit; DR Congo-Uzbekistan under direction missed badly because must-win acceleration and weak-defense collapse were underweighted.
common_diagnosis.market_layer: Market favorite direction misled Colombia-Portugal, but handicap protection signaled Colombia +1 value; market helped DR Congo direction and -1 cover tail more than the model absorbed.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.03 to +0.05 draw probability and +0.02 to +0.04 0-0/1-1 cluster when a group leader secures first place with draw and has strong defensive control.
parameter_updates.cap: single shift <= 0.05; do not apply when opponent scores early or leader must improve goal difference.
parameter_updates.reason: Colombia 0-0 Portugal was a clean draw-enough control outcome.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: -0.05 to -0.10 favorite xG when must-win favorite faces an opponent whose draw is strategically sufficient and defensive score >= 75.
parameter_updates.cap: do not reduce favorite win probability by more than 0.04 from this parameter alone.
parameter_updates.reason: Portugal pressure did not translate into enough chance quality against Colombia.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.02 to +0.04 draw and low-score matrix mass when market draw probability is close to or above model draw and context supports low risk.
parameter_updates.reason: Market draw signal in Colombia-Portugal was usable but underweighted.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.04 to +0.06 into 3-0/3-1/4-1 tail when a stronger team must win for qualification and opponent is theoretical-only or structurally exposed.
parameter_updates.cap: tail redistribution, not more than +0.03 to 1X2 direction unless market agrees.
parameter_updates.reason: DR Congo 3-1 Uzbekistan exposed a missing second-half tail.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.03 to +0.05 two-plus-margin bucket against teams with severe negative goal difference and prior multi-goal concessions.
parameter_updates.cap: only active after pre-match defensive fragility and game-state pressure both trigger.
parameter_updates.reason: Uzbekistan entered with heavy defensive damage and conceded the two-goal margin.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.02 to +0.04 BTTS yes when underdog has a named striker/transition route and must chase after conceding.
parameter_updates.cap: BTTS floor normally <= 0.52 unless underdog xG >= 0.80.
parameter_updates.reason: Uzbekistan still scored in a game where DR Congo cover tail hit.

parameter_updates.proposed_delta: schema/algorithm split: evaluate ordinary 1X2 favorite signal separately from handicap protection or cover signal.
parameter_updates.reason: Colombia +1 protection and DR Congo -1 cover signals were more informative than ordinary 1X2 alone.

player_state_backtest_link.note: The user requested model backtest output only; data-thread player-state normalization is not assumed complete here.

quality_notes: No betting advice is included; odds are market calibration only.
Actual scores were supplied by the main thread.
Output is ASCII-only to avoid encoding artifacts in this workspace shell.
